Kanan Dume Biofilter

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  was  identified  in  the  City’s  Enhanced Watershed Management Plan (EWMP).

b691e648-8c55-4f9c-a350-836b461a72ec.label: text_chunk
b691e648-8c55-4f9c-a350-836b461a72ec.text: This project includes the installation of  a  biofilter  on  Kanan  Dume  Drive  to  capture  and  treat  stormwater.  The project  is  intended  to  assist  with  Municipal  Separate  Storm  Sewer  System (MS4) Discharge Permit requirements.

PCH Median Improvements at Paradise Cove and Zuma Beach

¾ Project Description:

  This project was identified in the 2015 PCH Safety Study and includes installing new raised medians and improvements.  New raised medians are proposed east and west of PCH and Paradise Cove Road.

bb5d43c5-d4cc-476a-81d1-8e350237354c.label: text_chunk
bb5d43c5-d4cc-476a-81d1-8e350237354c.text:
The proposed improvements also include the relocation of the existing bus stop and new signage.

  The project will also include the installation of new raised medians on PCH in the areas where the double yellow lines exist in the vicinity of Zuma Beach, specifically where the yellow paddles are installed.

ef9d5ca9-d9fe-43ea-9fb2-16444077158e.text:
Capital Improvement Projects (Not Started)

PCH Crosswalk Improvements at Big Rock Drive and 20326 PCH ¾ Project Description:

 This project was identified in the 2015 PCH Safety Study and includes the installation of overhead warning signs and raised medians on PCH. New flashing warning beacons will be installed approximately 200 feet east and west of 20326 PCH (Moonshadow’s Restaurant).

bc8bfabe-0755-494e-a63a-fbf2ae14db7a.label: text_chunk
bc8bfabe-0755-494e-a63a-fbf2ae14db7a.text:
New raised medians, along with a pedestrian refuge area, are planned for this area. A new overhead flashing “signal ahead” beacon will be installed 400 feet east of PCH and Big Rock Drive intersection.

¾ Project Updates:  Staff continues to work with Measure M to finalize the scope of work.

Staff has also  prepared a Public Works  Quarterly Update flier.

bd33fc8b-51e7-4f26-99b3-c9b7151c23e5.label: text_chunk
bd33fc8b-51e7-4f26-99b3-c9b7151c23e5.text: This flier will include  a variety  of  Public  Works  statistics,  including  Projects  Under  Design,  Citizen  Request, Encroachment Permits Issued, and the amount of Development Reviews performed.  The flier  will  also  include  a  “Project  Spotlight”  that  will  describe  a  City  capital  improvement project.

**Summary of Relationships Involving the City:**

1. **Disaster Recovery Projects**: The City manages and oversees projects aimed at restoring services and infrastructure following disasters, highlighting its critical role in disaster management.

5. **Funding from Measure M**: The City has secured funding from Measure M, which provides financial resources for its projects, illustrating the financial support that underpins the City’s initiatives.

6. **Scheduled Meetings with City Council**: The City is set to meet with the City Council on July 10, 2023, to present matters for approval, reflecting the procedural dynamics between the City and its legislative body.

Overall, these relationships underscore the City's multifaceted role in managing projects, collaborating with the City Council, securing funding, and initiating community improvements.

The relationships highlight the impact of the Woolsey Fire on local infrastructure and the subsequent repair projects initiated in response. 

1. **Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements**: This project is aimed at repairing the damage inflicted by the Woolsey Fire, indicating a direct response to the fire's destructive effects on the drainage system.

2. **Point Dume Decomposed Granite Walkway Repair Project**: Similarly, this project was initiated due to the damage caused by the Woolsey Fire to the sidewalks, emphasizing the fire's widespread impact on community pathways.

In summary, both repair projects—the Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements and the Point Dume Decomposed Granite Walkway Repair—are direct responses to the damage caused by the Woolsey Fire, underscoring the fire's significant impact on local infrastructure.
